title: Add regression coverage for invalid transaction flows and integrity enforcement
summary: Add regression coverage for the v0.4.13 transaction state machine hardening work so specification-violating transaction flows are detected early, rejected deterministically, and logged with actionable integrity diagnostics. The checks should lock the intended behavior for lifecycle ordering, resume semantics, verify/commit sequencing, rebuild integrity, and event/state synchronization across the canonical transaction workflow.
outputs: Regression checks for invalid transaction flow detection and rejection
Regression checks for synchronized event-log and tx-state behavior across failure paths
Regression checks for deterministic resume, rebuild, and terminal-state handling
Verification evidence that v0.4.13 integrity rules remain enforced
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover rejection of lifecycle operations that occur before a canonical tx.begin.
Tests cover rejection of duplicate tx.begin for a non-terminal transaction and confirm resume does not emit a second begin.
Tests cover rejection of tx.verify.pass or tx.verify.fail when no matching tx.verify.start exists for the step.
Tests cover rejection of tx.commit.start when canonical tx.verify.pass has not been established.
Tests cover detection of event/state divergence after tx.begin, tx.verify.start, and tx.verify.pass.
Tests cover active transaction reconstruction failure or explicit integrity signaling when rebuild encounters invalid ordering such as duplicate non-terminal begin.
Tests cover rejection of non-terminal follow-up events after tx.end.done or tx.end.blocked.
Tests cover integrity diagnostics and structured error logging strongly enough to identify ticket id, transaction id, sequence context, and validation point from .agent artifacts.
Repository verification passes with the new regression coverage in place.
tasks: Identify the existing test modules that should own invalid-flow, synchronization-drift, rebuild-integrity, and diagnostics coverage.
Add or extend tests for lifecycle-before-begin and duplicate-begin violations using canonical event ordering assertions.
Add or extend tests for verify-result-without-start and commit-without-verify-pass violations.
Add or extend tests for resume behavior so existing non-terminal transactions are reused rather than re-begun.
Add or extend tests for rebuild behavior so invalid event sequences fail explicitly and do not silently collapse into a default empty active transaction.
Add or extend tests for post-terminal event rejection and repeated recovery attempts that must not multiply invalid transaction sequences.
Add or extend tests for structured integrity failure logging in .agent/errors.jsonl and related transaction-state artifacts.
Run verification and confirm the v0.4.13 transaction state machine contract is protected by automated checks.
notes: Keep the checks focused on enforcing the explicit v0.4.13 transaction state machine, not on weakening guards to accommodate invalid flows.
Prefer tests that assert canonical transaction behavior directly through tx_event_log, tx_state, rebuild, and guarded-operation outcomes.

Use the observed workflow violations as concrete regression targets: lifecycle before begin, stale verify state, commit without canonical verify pass, duplicate non-terminal begin, and active transaction loss during rebuild.
This ticket should confirm that invalid transaction flows fail early, fail specifically, and do not cascade into misleading downstream guard errors.
